pessimist.name: Sal Marusiak
pessimist.column:
The Mets lost again. Not in a dramatic, heart-wrenching way—no, this was the kind of loss that makes you want to throw your coffee cup at the TV. A 1-0 defeat to the A’s, a team that’s been struggling to stay above .500 all year. You’d think a team with the payroll of a mid-tier college football program could at least field a competent pitching staff. But no, the Mets are still doing that thing where they take a game they’re supposed to dominate and turn it into a nail-biter.  

Let’s talk about the pitching. The starter, whoever it was, lasted six innings and gave up one run. That’s not a bad line on paper. But here’s the thing: the Mets have been doing this all season. They’re the kind of team that can’t seem to figure out how to finish a game. You know the script: starter gets rocked in the first, bullpen can’t close the door, and somehow the other team wins. It’s like they’re playing a different sport. Maybe a sport where the rules are written by the opposition.  

And the defense? Oh, the defense. You’d think with all the money spent on free agents, they’d have a competent group in the field. But no, we
